topics.label: Linked List

problems.title: Insert at Head
problems.description: Given the head of a linked list and an integer val, insert a new node with that value at the beginning of the list and return the new head.
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [0, 1000]
problems.hint: Create a new node, point its next to the current head, return the new node.

problems.title: Insert at Tail
problems.description: Given the head of a linked list and an integer val, insert a new node with that value at the end of the list and return the head.
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [0, 1000]
problems.hint: Traverse to the last node, then attach the new node. Handle empty list as a special case.

problems.title: Insert at Index
problems.description: Given the head of a linked list, a 0-based index, and an integer val, insert a new node with that value at the given index and return the head. If index equals the list length, append to the end.
problems.hint: Walk to the node at (index - 1), then rewire pointers. Handle index 0 separately.

problems.title: Delete Head
problems.description: Given the head of a linked list, delete the head node and return the new head. If the list is empty, return null.
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [0, 1000]
problems.hint: Simply return head.next. Don't forget to handle the empty list case.

problems.title: Delete at Index
problems.description: Given the head of a linked list and a 0-based index, delete the node at that index and return the head.
constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [1, 1000]
problems.hint: Use a dummy node before head so head deletion needs no special case. Walk to (index-1) and rewire.

problems.title: Reverse Linked List
problems.description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return the reversed list.
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [0, 5000]
problems.hint: Keep track of prev, curr, and next pointers. At each step, reverse the link and advance all three.

problems.title: Find Middle of Linked List
problems.description: Given the head of a singly linked list, return the middle node. If there are two middle nodes, return the second middle node.
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [1, 100]
problems.hint: Use slow and fast pointers. Fast moves 2 steps, slow moves 1. When fast reaches end, slow is at middle.

problems.title: Detect Cycle
problems.description: Given the head of a linked list, determine if the linked list has a cycle in it. Return true if there is a cycle, otherwise return false.

examples.output: true  (tail connects to node at index 1)
true  (tail connects to node at index 0)

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [0, 10^4]
problems.hint: Floyd's algorithm: use a slow pointer (1 step) and fast pointer (2 steps). If they meet, there's a cycle.

problems.title: Floyd's Cycle Detection
problems.description: Given the head of a linked list, return the node where the cycle begins. If there is no cycle, return null. You must solve it with O(1) memory.
constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [0, 10^4]
problems.hint: Phase 1: detect cycle with slow/fast. Phase 2: reset one pointer to head, move both 1 step at a time — they meet at cycle entry.
